# Can a belief be justified if created by unreliable processes?

**Node Type:** antithesis
**Node ID:** 8acd5515-673a-4a73-8ae3-2768c3b57340
**Depth:** 4
**Terminal:** No

**Parent:** [Reliabilist Coherentism](reliabilist-coherentism-synthesis-d6c1c642-66ac-40b0-82ca-187cf7250017.md)

## Content

**Suppose we have a case in which a belief is generated by an unreliable process that occasionally produces true beliefs. Intuitively, this belief should not be considered justified even if it happens to be true.**, **It is implausible that a belief can be justified if the cognitive process that produced it is known to be generally unreliable.**
